title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a memory leak vulnerability in the VIFF encoder when memory...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a memory leak vulnerability in the VIFF encoder when memory allocation fails. Attackers can trigger allocation failures by processing specially crafted VIFF images to exhaust available memory and cause denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61870.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a use-after-free vulnerability in the FormatMagickCaption...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a use-after-free vulnerability in the FormatMagickCaption method when memory allocation fails. Attackers can trigger memory allocation failures to cause a dangling pointer to reference freed memory, potentially enabling denial of service or code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use-after-free vulnerability in ImageMagick can allow attackers to exploit memory allocation failures, potentially leading to denial of service or remote code execution. This poses a significant risk to systems processing untrusted image files.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ImageMagick to version 7.1.2-26 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for image files processed by ImageMagick to reduce the risk of exploitation.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or crashes that could indicate exploitation attempts, and apply runtime protections if possible.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-19 contains a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the magnify...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-19 contains a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the magnify operation that allows attackers to read out of bounds memory. An unrecognized magnify:method value triggers an out of bounds read, potentially exposing sensitive information or causing denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The heap buffer overflow vulnerability in ImageMagick's magnify operation could allow attackers to read out-of-bounds memory, potentially exposing sensitive information or causing a denial of service. This could lead to data breaches or service disruptions if exploited.
recommended_actions: Update ImageMagick to version 7.1.2-19 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit to identify and mitigate any other potential vulnerabilities in the software stack.
Implement input validation and sandboxing to limit the impact of potential exploitation.

title: Parse Server is affected by a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in versions >= 9.0...
description: Parse Server is affected by a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in versions >= 9.0.0, < 9.10.0-alpha.2 and <= 8.6.83. When an uploaded file's extension is not recognized by the mime package, Parse Server preserves the client-supplied Content-Type. A malformed Content-Type that is not a valid type/subtype media type (e.g., 'image', 'image/', or 'image//svg+xml') bypasses the fileUpload.fileExtensions blocklist and is stored unchanged. On storage adapters that persist and serve the u

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61448.

title: PraisonAI before 1.6.78 contains a remote code execution vulnerability in CodeAgent...
description: PraisonAI before 1.6.78 contains a remote code execution vulnerability in CodeAgent._execute_python() that executes LLM-generated Python code without AST validation, import restrictions, or sandbox enforcement. Attackers can influence LLM output through prompt injection to exfiltrate all environment secrets and execute arbitrary code on the host system.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61447.

title: The Grav Admin2 plugin (getgrav/grav-plugin-admin2) before 2.0.4 embeds a global JavaScript...
description: The Grav Admin2 plugin (getgrav/grav-plugin-admin2) before 2.0.4 embeds a global JavaScript variable window.__GRAV_CONFIG__ in the Admin2 SPA bootstrap page at /grav/admin (and its subroutes). This object is returned in every unauthenticated response and discloses the server URL, API prefix, admin base path, runtime environment type, and exact Grav and Admin2 version numbers, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to fingerprint the deployment and select version-specific exploits without reconnais

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Grav Admin2 plugin allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive configuration details, such as server URL, API prefix, admin base path, runtime environment type, and exact software versions. This information can be used to fingerprint the system and launch version-specific exploits, increasing the risk of targeted attacks, data breaches, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Update the Grav Admin2 plugin to version 2.0.4 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to monitor and block unauthorized access attempts.
Regularly review and restrict access to sensitive configuration files and endpoints to authenticated users only.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a heap use-after-free vulnerability caused by missing null...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a heap use-after-free vulnerability caused by missing null check when parsing XMP profiles. Attackers can craft malicious image files with specially crafted XMP data to trigger the vulnerability and cause application crashes.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ImageMagick could allow attackers to craft malicious image files with specially crafted XMP data, potentially leading to application crashes or further exploitation such as remote code execution in certain scenarios. This could disrupt services relying on image processing.
recommended_actions: Update ImageMagick to version 7.1.2-26 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization for uploaded image files to mitigate potential exploitation.
Monitor systems for unusual activity related to image processing and review logs for signs of exploitation.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 and 6.9.13-51 is missing a check for the allowed memory allocation...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 and 6.9.13-51 is missing a check for the allowed memory allocation limit in matrix-backed operations such as -canny. An attacker can supply a crafted image that causes ImageMagick to allocate more memory than permitted by the configured policy, resulting in a denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61465.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a policy bypass vulnerability in the APNG encoder and...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a policy bypass vulnerability in the APNG encoder and external delegates due to missing validation checks. Attackers can write files to disallowed paths by bypassing configured policy restrictions through the APNG encoding process.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61858.

title: PraisonAI Platform (praisonai-platform) before 0.1.9 fails to enforce owner/admin authorization...
description: PraisonAI Platform (praisonai-platform) before 0.1.9 fails to enforce owner/admin authorization on the PATCH routes for projects, issues, and agents, which only require workspace-member role. A workspace member can modify owner-created records; for projects, a member can reassign lead_id to their own user id and then delete the owner-created project, bypassing the delete route's owner/admin permission check.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61442.

title: PraisonAI before 4.6.78 fails to validate file path references in custom command templates,...
description: PraisonAI before 4.6.78 fails to validate file path references in custom command templates, allowing attackers to read files outside the workspace. Attackers can include path traversal sequences like @../outside_secret.txt or absolute paths in project command files to exfiltrate process-readable files into model prompts.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit path traversal in custom command templates, potentially leading to unauthorized access and exfiltration of sensitive files. This could compromise confidentiality and integrity, especially if sensitive or proprietary data is exposed.
recommended_actions: Upgrade PraisonAI to version 4.6.78 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for file path references in custom command templates to prevent path traversal.
Conduct a security audit to identify and mitigate any unauthorized access or data exfiltration that may have occurred.

title: PraisonAI before 4.6.78 contains arbitrary file write and command execution vulnerabilities in...
description: PraisonAI before 4.6.78 contains arbitrary file write and command execution vulnerabilities in the AICoder component due to missing path validation and command sanitization in LLM tool calls. Attackers can inject malicious prompts through the chat interface to write files to arbitrary filesystem locations and execute arbitrary shell commands with root privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary shell commands with root privileges and write files to any location on the filesystem, potentially leading to full system compromise, data exfiltration, and disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade PraisonAI to version 4.6.78 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user inputs in the AICoder component to prevent malicious prompt injection.
Conduct a thorough security audit of systems running PraisonAI to identify potential signs of exploitation and mitigate any residual risks.

title: PraisonAI versions before 4.6.78 contain a prompt injection defense misconfiguration where the...
description: PraisonAI versions before 4.6.78 contain a prompt injection defense misconfiguration where the block threshold defaults to CRITICAL severity, allowing HIGH-level threats to pass through unblocked. Attackers can submit single-vector prompt injection attacks such as instruction overrides or financial manipulation that trigger HIGH severity detection but are logged without blocking, enabling system prompt extraction and unauthorized tool invocations.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61439.

title: PraisonAI versions before 1.6.78 contain a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the...
description: PraisonAI versions before 1.6.78 contain a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the Crawl4AI/Chromium backend that allows attackers to bypass SSRF validation by exploiting DNS rebinding and HTTP redirects. Attackers can craft URLs that resolve to internal services after the initial validation check, enabling the headless browser to follow redirects and read internal responses including sensitive canary values.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SSRF vulnerability in PraisonAI versions before 1.6.78 allows attackers to bypass validation mechanisms using DNS rebinding and HTTP redirects. This can lead to unauthorized access to internal services, exposure of sensitive data, and potential lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to PraisonAI version 1.6.78 or later, as the vulnerability has been patched in this release.
Implement network-level protections such as firewall rules to restrict access to internal services from untrusted sources.
Conduct a thorough audit of internal systems to identify and mitigate any potential exposure caused by this vulnerability.

title: PraisonAI AgentMail versions before 4.6.78 lack signature verification in webhook mode, allowing...
description: PraisonAI AgentMail versions before 4.6.78 lack signature verification in webhook mode, allowing unauthenticated attackers to inject messages with spoofed sender addresses. Attackers can POST crafted message.received events to the webhook endpoint to inject arbitrary content into the agent and trigger replies to attacker-controlled addresses, bypassing sender allow/block lists.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61428.

title: Hono before 4.12.7 allows __proto__ key in parseBody with dot option enabled, permitting...
description: Hono before 4.12.7 allows __proto__ key in parseBody with dot option enabled, permitting specially crafted form field names to create objects with __proto__ properties. When parsed results are merged into regular JavaScript objects using unsafe merge patterns, attackers can exploit this to achieve prototype pollution and modify object behavior.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56763.

title: PraisonAI before 4.6.78 fails to validate the caller-controlled dimension argument in the...
description: PraisonAI before 4.6.78 fails to validate the caller-controlled dimension argument in the PGVector and Cassandra knowledge-store create_collection() backends. Although schema, keyspace, and collection-name identifiers are validated, the dimension value (declared as int but not enforced at runtime) is interpolated directly into the vector column of the generated CREATE TABLE DDL. A caller able to influence collection-creation dimensions can pass a string such as '3); DROP TABLE tenant_secrets; --

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious SQL commands via the dimension argument, potentially leading to unauthorized data manipulation or deletion, including sensitive tables such as tenant_secrets.
recommended_actions: Immediately update PraisonAI to version 4.6.78 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user-controlled inputs, especially in database-related operations.
Conduct a security audit to identify and mitigate any potential damage caused by exploitation of this vulnerability.

title: PraisonAI before 1.7.3 contains an insecure default configuration that binds to all interfaces...
description: PraisonAI before 1.7.3 contains an insecure default configuration that binds to all interfaces with no API key requirement and wildcard CORS. Unauthenticated attackers can call GET /api/agents to read agent instructions and system prompts, or POST /api/chat to invoke agents without authentication.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61426.

title: Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an information disclosure vulnerability in the...
description: Capgo before 12.128.2 contains an information disclosure vulnerability in the find_apikey_by_value PostgreSQL function marked SECURITY DEFINER and executable by the anon role. Unauthenticated attackers can call this function via the /rest/v1/rpc/find_apikey_by_value endpoint to retrieve sensitive API key metadata including user_id, mode, org scoping, and expiration details when supplied a valid key value.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to exploit a SECURITY DEFINER PostgreSQL function to retrieve sensitive API key metadata, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, and privilege escalation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Capgo version 12.128.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Audit and restrict access to the /rest/v1/rpc/find_apikey_by_value endpoint to prevent unauthorized calls.
Review and rotate all API keys that may have been exposed to ensure no unauthorized access persists.

title: Capgo before 12.128.12 contains a billing authorization bypass vulnerability in the plan_valid...
description: Capgo before 12.128.12 contains a billing authorization bypass vulnerability in the plan_valid calculation that allows organizations with exhausted or expired usage credit grants to bypass billing gates. Attackers can exploit the divergence between the plugin hot-path plan_valid expression and the authoritative billing gate to gain continued access to /updates, /stats, /channel_self, and attachment upload endpoints after credit depletion.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows organizations with depleted or expired usage credits to bypass billing restrictions and continue accessing critical endpoints such as updates, stats, and attachment uploads. This could lead to unauthorized resource usage, financial losses, and potential service abuse.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Capgo version 12.128.12 or later to patch the billing authorization bypass vulnerability.
Review and audit billing and authorization mechanisms to ensure consistency between the plugin hot-path and authoritative billing gate.
Monitor usage patterns for anomalies that may indicate exploitation of this vulnerability and take immediate action if detected.

title: CVE-2025-71372 - Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __r
description: Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __reduce__ methods, allowing arbitrary code execution. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files that execute arbitrary Python code when loaded, bypassing Picklescan's safety checks and enabling supply-chain poisoning of shared model files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71372.

title: CVE-2025-71369 - picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.
description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.utils.decoder.basichandlers in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected malicious code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71369.

title: CVE-2025-71367 - picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect _operator.attrgetter function calls in pickle payloads, all
description: picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect _operator.attrgetter function calls in pickle payloads, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files using _operator.attrgetter in reduce methods to execute arbitrary code when pickle.load() processes the file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71367.

description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious torch.utils.bottleneck.__main__.run_cprofile function calls in pickle files, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files to achieve arbitrary code execution when victims load the files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71366.

description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect the asyncio.unix_events._UnixSubprocessTransport._start function in pickle reduce methods, allowing remote code execution. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files embedding this built-in function that evade detection but execute arbitrary commands when loaded.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71364.

title: CVE-2025-71362 - picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect unsafe deserialization when numpy.f2py.crackfortran functio
description: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect unsafe deserialization when numpy.f2py.crackfortran functions call eval on arbitrary strings. Attackers can embed malicious code in pickle files that executes when loaded from untrusted sources.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71362.

description: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect malicious pickle files using idlelib.calltip.get_entity function in reduce methods. Attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes remote commands when loaded by victims.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71360.

description: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect malicious pickle payloads that utilize lib2to3.pgen2.grammar.Grammar.loads in the reduce method, allowing remote code execution. Attackers can craft pickle files embedding dangerous code that evades picklescan detection and executes during pickle.load() deserialization.

description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes.ShapeEnv.evaluate_guards_expression function calls in pickle files. Attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes remote code when loaded by victims.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71356.

description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that exploit torch._dynamo.guards.GuardBuilder.get function in reduce methods. Attackers can craft pickle files with embedded code that evades picklescan detection and executes arbitrary commands when loaded.

description: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect malicious pickle files using numpy.f2py.crackfortran.param_eval function in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling arbitrary code execution in applications loading untrusted pickle data.

description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect malicious pickle files that invoke torch.utils.bottleneck.__main__.run_autograd_prof function. Attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling remote code execution.

description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect malicious pickle files that exploit lib2to3.pgen2.pgen.ParserGenerator.make_label function in the reduce method. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files with embedded code that evades detection but executes arbitrary commands when pickle.load() is called.

description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect malicious pickle files using idlelib.run.Executive.runcode in reduce methods. Attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes during pickle.load, enabling remote code execution in PyTorch models and supply chain attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71342.

title: CVE-2026-54424 - An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potent
description: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potential Elevation of Privilege. This issue affects Parsec through v2026-05-04.0. The patched version is Parsec for Windows version 150-104a. A user can generate a situation where there is an instance of parsecd.exe running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM with a user-controlled value of the AppData environment variable.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54424.
